root_text.an5.153:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato suṇantopi saddhammaṁ abhabbo niyāmaṁ okkamituṁ kusalesu dhammesu sammattaṁ.
root_text.an5.153:1.3: Makkhī dhammaṁ suṇāti makkhapariyuṭṭhito, upārambhacitto dhammaṁ suṇāti randhagavesī, dhammadesake āhatacitto hoti khīlajāto, duppañño hoti jaḷo eḷamūgo, anaññāte aññātamānī hoti.
root_text.an5.153:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato suṇantopi saddhammaṁ abhabbo niyāmaṁ okkamituṁ kusalesu dhammesu sammattaṁ.
root_text.an5.153:2.1: Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato suṇanto saddhammaṁ bhabbo niyāmaṁ okkamituṁ kusalesu dhammesu sammattaṁ.
root_text.an5.153:2.3: Amakkhī dhammaṁ suṇāti na makkhapariyuṭṭhito, anupārambhacitto dhammaṁ suṇāti na randhagavesī, dhammadesake anāhatacitto hoti akhīlajāto, paññavā hoti ajaḷo aneḷamūgo, na anaññāte aññātamānī hoti.
root_text.an5.153:2.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato suṇanto saddhammaṁ bhabbo niyāmaṁ okkamituṁ kusalesu dhammesu sammattan”ti.

translation_text.an5.153:1.1: “Mendicants, someone with five qualities is unable to step into the sure path with regards to skillful qualities even when listening to the true teaching.
translation_text.an5.153:1.3: They listen to the teaching bent only on putting it down. They listen to the teaching with a hostile, fault-finding mind. They’re antagonistic to the teacher, planning to attack them. They’re witless, dull, and idiotic. And they think they know what they don’t know.
translation_text.an5.153:1.4: Someone with these five qualities is unable to step into the sure path with regards to skillful qualities even when listening to the true teaching.
translation_text.an5.153:2.1: Someone with five qualities is able to step into the sure path with regards to skillful qualities when listening to the true teaching.
translation_text.an5.153:2.3: They don’t listen to the teaching bent only on putting it down. They don’t listen to the teaching with a hostile, fault-finding mind. They’re not antagonistic to the teacher, and not planning to attack them. They’re wise, bright, and clever. And they don’t think they know what they don’t know.
translation_text.an5.153:2.4: Someone with these five qualities is able to step into the sure path with regards to skillful qualities when listening to the true teaching.”

variant_text.an5.153:1.3: upārambhacitto → saupārambhacitto (sya-all, km) | khīlajāto → khilajāto (bj, sya-all, pts1ed)
